ground_truth.title: [Vocabulary] - Car rental or car rent? | UsingEnglish.com ESL Forum
ground_truth.author: tomashusner@hotmail.com
ground_truth.main_content: The original poster, Tomas from the Czech Republic, is developing an English course for car rental companies and asks which sounds better: 'Car rental English' or 'English for car rental'. Forum members respond with various suggestions including 'An English Course in Car Rental', 'English for Car Rental businesses', and 'English for Car Rental Companies'. The discussion also covers regional English differences - in British English, 'car hire' is commonly used (e.g., 'Car Hire Agency') while American English prefers 'car rental' and 'rent a car'. One British speaker clarifies they would use 'car hire' as the noun and 'rent a car' as the verb. Another suggestion focuses on the target audience: 'English for Car Rental Personnels/Professionals' rather than businesses.
with: The original poster, Tomas from the Czech Republic, is developing an English course for car rental companies and asks which sounds better: 'Car rental English' or 'English for car rental'.
